intro.title: $FIG
intro.description: Once the company I worked for (Sigma) went public, it seemed like my mood at work started to heavily depend on the trends of the market. I started to resent how much this one magical number controlled my life and my future.

events.title: $FIG goes public
events.description: The best day of my career. I went to Wall Street to watch the opening bell ring and the stock price climb to a number higher than any of us thought possible. I told my coworker $FIG hit $100 as a joke, but it was true by the end of the day. I went to the afterparty surrounded by people who were now worth millions (or in the case of the CEO) billions of dollars.

events.title: The first opportunity to sell
events.description: By the time the first lockup expired, sentiment was still high, but the stock price was down. I believed in the future of Figma, and have regretted not selling every day for the past 6 months.

events.title: Passed up for promotion.
events.description: The stock hit an all time low, even lower than the IPO price of $33. I had seen so much money slip through my fingers, and my morale was low. It sunk even lower when I was informed by my manager that I would not be getting the long-awaited promo that I hoped for. I started to silently hope that the company go through a round of lay-offs.

events.title: Submitted my two weeks
events.description: Unable to work from China or take more than two weeks off of work, I submitted my notice so that I could attend my final semester of NYU in Shanghai. I felt much more nostalgic than I had anticipated, and still cant believe that five year chapter of my life is over.
